research_series.series_name: Uncertainty Scoring,Confidence Calibration,and Health State Inference

patterns.pattern_name: Clinical and digital health models often report discrimination without adequate calibration

mediator.name: calibration_error
mediator.pathway: model score does not reliably equal observed probability
mediator.mechanism: lack of calibration plots/metrics, external validation, dataset shift, measurement noise

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Do not present an uncalibrated model score as probability; require calibration metrics and external validation before using it as confidence.

hypotheses.reason: Systematic reviews show calibration is poorly reported and many ML clinical models have high risk of bias.

evidence.summary: Calibration reporting is inadequate in most published clinical prediction models; discrimination metrics are common but confidence/probability calibration is often missing.

key_sources.title: Calibrated Confidence, Uncertainty Scoring, and Health State Inference in Human Health
key_sources.relevant_finding: <25% of many published models report calibration plots/metrics; >90% report AUC/C-index.
key_sources.limitations: Review-level synthesis; outcome-specific fatigue/brain fog calibration remains sparse.

limitations: Calibration data for fatigue/brain fog/wearable inference is sparse.
Digital health studies often lack external validation.
alternative_explanations: High discrimination may coexist with poor calibration.
Model performance may degrade under dataset shift.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Wearable/digital health model confidence can be treated as calibrated probability.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Most wearable/digital health studies lack robust calibration reporting.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Calibrated confidence assigned to an inferred state after accounting for evidence, measurement reliability, missingness, context and validation status.
Risk that an inference is stated more confidently than supported by calibrated evidence.

research_gaps: Outcome-specific calibration for fatigue, brain fog, recovery and wearable inference.
Routine epistemic/aleatoric separation in non-imaging health data.

most_supported_patterns: Calibration reporting is inadequate in many clinical prediction models.
Wearable/digital health inference often lacks robust uncertainty calibration.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: Separate model confidence from true probability.
patterns_requiring_caution: AUC/C-index should not be treated as calibration.
Bayesian/conformal methods are promising but not universally adopted.
recommended_next_queries: How to operationalize uncertainty scoring for BodyState Mapper graph edges?
summary.overall_assessment: Strong methodological guardrail series; include as core inference-confidence layer.

works_cited.title: Calibrated Confidence, Uncertainty Scoring, and Health State Inference in Human Health
